name: Foster Botanical Garden
tagline: Honolulu's oldest botanical garden (1853) — ancient trees, rare orchids, and a peaceful escape in the heart of the city.
bestTime: Weekday mornings for the most peaceful experience
talkStory: Founded in 1853 when German botanist William Hillebrand planted the first specimens on land leased from Queen Kalama, making it the oldest botanical garden in Hawaii. The garden's sacred bo tree was grown from a cutting of the very tree in Sri Lanka under which Siddhartha Gautama attained enlightenment. Several trees here are the last living specimens of their species on Earth — the garden quietly serves as a conservation ark. The prehistoric glen features cycads and ferns whose lineage predates the dinosaurs by 100 million years.
